questions.prompt_html: If  = -32, find k.
questions.prompt_text: If (x →k) is under (lim) ((x) with superscript (4) -( k) with superscript (4)/x-k) = -32, find k.
questions.solution_html:
First observe that x4 - k4 = (x2 - k2 )(x2 + k2 ) = (x - k)(x + k)(x2 + k2 )
Hence, =  = (x + k)(x2 + k2 ) = (2k)(2k2) = 4k3
Now 4k3 = -32 ⇒ k3 = -8, therefore k =  = -2.

questions.prompt_html:
Use the formal definition of the limit to verify the addition property of limits: 

If f(x) = L and g(x) = K, then [f(x) + g(x)] = L + K.
questions.prompt_text: Use the formal definition of the limit to verify the addition property of limits: If (x → a) is under (lim) f(x) = L and (x → a) is under (lim) g(x) = K, then (x → a) is under (lim) [f(x) + g(x)] = L + K.
questions.solution_html:
Let ε > 0. Since f(x) = L, there exists δ1 such that if 0 < |x - a| < δ1, then 
|f(x) - L| < . Since g(x) = K, there exists δ2 such that if 0 < |x - a| < δ2, then 
|g(x) - K| < . If 0 < |x - a| < δ = min{δ1, δ2}, then 
|(f(x) + g(x)) - (L + K)| ≤ |f(x) - L| + |g(x) - K| <  +  = ε. Thus (f(x) + g(x)) = L + K.
questions.solution_text: Let ε > 0. Since (x → a) is under (lim) f(x) = L, there exists δ 1 such that if 0 < |x - a| < δ 1 , then |f(x) - L| < (ε/2) . Since (x → a) is under (lim) g(x) = K, there exists δ 2 such that if 0 < |x - a| < δ 2 , then |g(x) - K| < (ε/2) . If 0 < |x - a| < δ = min{δ 1 , δ 2 }, then |(f(x) + g(x)) - (L + K)| ≤ |f(x) - L| + |g(x) - K| < (ε/2) + (ε/2) = ε. Thus (x → a) is under (lim) (f(x) + g(x)) = L + K.
